mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS path lowers discount rates and lifts equity valuations, so declines in the policy rate over the prior 3 months predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS has declined over the prior 3 months, or if the rolling 3-year rank IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.3 percentage point or larger rise in UNRATE over 3 months signals a growth scare that tightens financial conditions and depresses risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not show negative 20-day forward returns more than 55% of the time, or if the average triggered return is not below the unconditional mean by at least 1 percentage point annualized.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and PAYEMS both accelerate, households and firms are simultaneously spending and hiring, indicating broad demand momentum that supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint-signal portfolio does not outperform its single-series components, or if 60-day forward returns are not positive in at least 55% of cases when both series are rising on a 3-month basis.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO relative to recent months indicates expanding real output and improving corporate revenue capacity, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO breakouts are not followed by above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal's hit rate is below 55% across triggered episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in HOUST signals a housing-led demand slowdown that weakens cyclical earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not negative in at least 55% of large HOUST drawdown episodes, or if the conditional mean return is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS indicates the Fed is tightening in response to inflation pressure, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint inflation-plus-rate signal does not produce negative 5-day returns more often than not, or if the interaction term is not stronger than either proxy alone.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth slows while UNRATE rises, labor market deterioration implies weaker household income and consumption, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month growth < 0.2% and UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the combined labor deterioration regime fails to produce negative 60-day returns in at least 55% of triggered cases, or if returns are indistinguishable from baseline at conventional significance levels.

mechanism_narrative: A positive GDP surprise after several months of weak readings signals that aggregate demand is reaccelerating, and this should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations improve.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 rises above its prior 2-quarter average
failure_condition: Reject if GDP reacceleration episodes are not followed by positive 60-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the conditional mean return does not exceed the unconditional mean by at least 1 percentage point annualized.
